claims.evidence_text: the gross irrigation requirement of MBD is 59.8 and net irrigation is 41.9 mm less than CWR which is 180 mm
claims.claim_summary: Total seasonal crop water requirement (CWR) of wheat at Mandibahauddin, Punjab is about 180 mm (CROPWAT/Penman-Monteith).

claims.evidence_text: The total irrigation water requirement of wheat in MBD, FSD, TTS, BWN, BWP, and KNP is 44.7, 102.2, 103.1, 119.1, 179, and 184.2 mm, respectively.
claims.claim_summary: Seasonal irrigation water requirement of wheat across Punjab agro-climatic zones ranges 44.7-184.2 mm (highest in arid southern districts).

claims.evidence_text: the highest IWR was observed in KNP 184.2 mm, as this area has a sub-tropical desert climate, less rainfall, and hot summers resulting in high water requirements and the lowest IWR was observed in MBD 44.7 mm
claims.claim_summary: Maximum wheat irrigation water requirement 184.2 mm at Khanpur (sub-tropical desert climate); minimum 44.7 mm at Mandibahauddin.
